component.name: Xeneon Edge Linux Hub
component.summary: Native widget dashboard for secondary touchscreen displays

description.p: Xeneon Edge Linux Hub turns a secondary touchscreen - such as the Corsair Xeneon Edge - into a persistent, touch-editable dashboard of live widgets. It runs natively on Linux (Qt 6 + a Rust core), with no browser and no server.

ul.li: Live system metrics straight from the kernel: CPU, GPU, memory, disk, network and temperatures
Focus/Pomodoro timer, tasks, habits, hydration and other ADHD-friendly productivity widgets
Clock, weather, calendar, moon phase, media controls, countdowns and more
Touch-editable layout: drag, resize, add and remove widgets across multiple pages
Per-widget appearance, themes, animated backgrounds and wallpapers
Automatic screen rotation on supported hardware

description.p: Configure it directly on the touchscreen, or from the companion Xeneon Edge Manager desktop app, which mirrors the layout live.
First signed alpha release, with curated presets, controlled network widgets, policy support, and hardened configuration and IPC handling.
Initial release: real widget dashboard, companion Manager, live metrics and touch editing.
